title: Greatest common factor of monomials
description: Find the greatest monomial that is a common factor of other monomials. For example, 2x is the greatest common factor of 4x and 6x².

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $3x^4$, $15x^3$, and $21x^2$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
3x^4&=(3)(x)(x)(x)(x)
\\\\
15x^3&=(3)(5)(x)(x)(x)
\\\\
21x^2&=(3)(7)(x)(x)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 3$ and two factors of $\blueD x$:

$\begin{align}
3x^4&=(\goldD 3)(\blueD x)(\blueD x)(x)(x)
\\\\
15x^3&=(\goldD 3)(5)(\blueD x)(\blueD x)(x)
\\\\
21x^2&=(\goldD 3)(7)(\blueD x)(\blueD x)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 3)(\blueD x)(\blueD x)=3x^2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $35y^{4}$, $14y^{4}$, and $63y^{4}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
35y^{4}&=(5)(7)(y)(y)(y)(y)
\\\\
14y^{4}&=(2)(7)(y)(y)(y)(y)
\\\\
63y^{4}&=(3)(3)(7)(y)(y)(y)(y)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 7$ and four factors of $\blueD y$:

$\begin{align}
35y^{4}&=(5)(\goldD 7)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)
\\\\
14y^{4}&=(2)(\goldD 7)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)
\\\\
63y^{4}&=(3)(3)(\goldD 7)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 7)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)(\blueD y)=7y^{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $6a$, $8a^{2}$, and $14$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
6a&=(2)(3)(a)
\\\\
8a^{2}&=(2)(2)(2)(a)(a)
\\\\
14&=(2)(7)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 2$.

$\begin{align}
6a&=(\goldD 2)(3)(a)
\\\\
8a^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(2)(2)(a)(a)
\\\\
14&=(\goldD 2)(7)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$\goldD 2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $35b^{2}$, $15b^{3}$, and $5b$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
35b^{2}&=(5)(7)(b)(b)
\\\\
15b^{3}&=(3)(5)(b)(b)(b)
\\\\
5b&=(5)(b)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 5$ and one factor of $\blueD b$:

$\begin{align}
35b^{2}&=(\goldD 5)(7)(\blueD b)(b)
\\\\
15b^{3}&=(3)(\goldD 5)(\blueD b)(b)(b)
\\\\
5b&=(\goldD 5)(\blueD b)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 5)(\blueD b)=5b$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $44c^{5}$, $22c^{3}$, and $11c^4$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
44c^{5}&=(11)(2)(2)(c)(c)(c)(c)(c)
\\\\
22c^{3}&=(11)(2)(c)(c)(c)
\\\\
11c^{4}&=(11)(c)(c)(c)(c)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD {11}$ and three factors of $\blueD c$:

$\begin{align}
44c^{5}&=(\goldD {11})(2)(2)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(c)(c)
\\\\
22c^{3}&=(\goldD {11})(2)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)
\\\\
11c^{4}&=(\goldD {11})(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(c)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD {11})(\blueD c)(\blueD c)(\blueD c)=11c^3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $8m^{2}$, $4m$, and $10m^4$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
8m^{2}&=(2)(2)(2)(m)(m)
\\\\
4m&=(2)(2)(m)
\\\\
10m^{4}&=(2)(5)(m)(m)(m)(m)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD {2}$ and one factor of $\blueD m$:

$\begin{align}
8m^{2}&=(\goldD2)(2)(2)(\blueD m)(m)
\\\\
4m&=(\goldD2)(2)(\blueD m)
\\\\
10m^{4}&=(\goldD2)(5)(\blueD m)(m)(m)(m)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD {2})(\blueD m)=2m$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $20a^{2}$, $12a^{2}$, and $8a$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
20a^{2}&=(2)(2)(5)(a)(a)
\\\\
12a^{2}&=(2)(2)(3)(a)(a)
\\\\
8a&=(2)(2)(2)(a)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have two factors of $\goldD 2$ and one factor of $\blueD a$:

$\begin{align}
20a^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(5)(\blueD a)(a)
\\\\
12a^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(3)(\blueD a)(a)
\\\\
8a&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(2)(\blueD a)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(\blueD a)=4a$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $30b^{2}$, $80b^{2}$, and $20b^{3}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
30b^{2}&=(2)(3)(5)(b)(b)
\\\\
80b^{2}&=(2)(2)(2)(2)(5)(b)(b)
\\\\
20b^{3}&=(2)(2)(5)(b)(b)(b)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 2$, one factor of $\blueD 5$, and two factors of $\maroonD b$:

$\begin{align}
30b^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(3)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD b)(\maroonD b)
\\\\
80b^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(2)(2)(2)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD b)(\maroonD b)
\\\\
20b^{3}&=(\goldD 2)(2)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD b)(\maroonD b)(b)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 2)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD b)(\maroonD b)=10b^{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $14c^{3}$, $70c^{4}$, and $28c^{2}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
14c^{3}&=(2)(7)(c)(c)(c)
\\\\
70c^{4}&=(2)(5)(7)(c)(c)(c)(c)
\\\\
28c^{2}&=(2)(2)(7)(c)(c)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 2$, one factor of $\blueD 7$, and two factors of $\maroonD c$:

$\begin{align}
14c^{3}&=(\goldD 2)(\blueD 7)(\maroonD c)(\maroonD c)(c)
\\\\
70c^{4}&=(\goldD 2)(5)(\blueD 7)(\maroonD c)(\maroonD c)(c)(c)
\\\\
28c^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(2)(\blueD 7)(\maroonD c)(\maroonD c)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 2)(\blueD 7)(\maroonD c)(\maroonD c)=14c^{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $54z^{3}$, $18z$, and $36z^{2}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
54z^{3}&=(2)(3)(3)(3)(z)(z)(z)
\\\\
18z&=(2)(3)(3)(z)
\\\\
36z^{2}&=(2)(2)(3)(3)(z)(z)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 2$, two factors of $\blueD 3$, and one factor of $\maroonD z$:

$\begin{align}
54z^{3}&=(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(\blueD 3)(3)(\maroonD z)(z)(z)
\\\\
18z&=(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(\blueD 3)(\maroonD z)
\\\\
36z^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(2)(\blueD 3)(\blueD 3)(\maroonD z)(z)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(\blueD 3)(\maroonD z)=18z$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $15n^{5}$, $30n^{3}$, and $45n^{2}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
15n^{5}&=(3)(5)(n)(n)(n)(n)(n)
\\\\
30n^{3}&=(2)(3)(5)(n)(n)(n)
\\\\
45n^{2}&=(3)(3)(5)(n)(n)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have one factor of $\goldD 3$, one factor of $\blueD 5$, and two factors of $\maroonD n$:

$\begin{align}
15n^{5}&=(\goldD 3)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD n)(\maroonD n)(n)(n)(n)
\\\\
30n^{3}&=(2)(\goldD 3)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD n)(\maroonD n)(n)
\\\\
45n^{2}&=(\goldD 3)(3)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD n)(\maroonD n)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 3)(\blueD 5)(\maroonD n)(\maroonD n)=15n^2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the greatest common factor of $60w$, $36w^{2}$, and $24w^{4}$?**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
Let's factor each monomial to its prime factors:

$\begin{align}
60w&=(2)(2)(3)(5)(w)
\\\\
36w^{2}&=(2)(2)(3)(3)(w)(w)
\\\\
24w^{4}&=(2)(2)(2)(3)(w)(w)(w)(w)
\end{align}$

We want the largest set of factors that's included in all three monomials.

All of the monomials have two factors of $\goldD 2$, one factor of $\blueD 3$, and one factor of $\maroonD w$:

$\begin{align}
60w&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(5)(\maroonD w)
\\\\
36w^{2}&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(3)(\maroonD w)(w)
\\\\
24w^{4}&=(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(2)(\blueD 3)(\maroonD w)(w)(w)(w)
\end{align}$

This is the greatest common factor:

$(\goldD 2)(\goldD 2)(\blueD 3)(\maroonD w)=12w$
